article.title: Nintendo's Tomodachi Life revival ships after a nine-year development cycle
article.summary: Nintendo says Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream launched on April 16, with development starting around 2017 and the game rebuilt around user-generated content.
article.body_markdown:
## Overview

Nintendo says *Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream* launched on April 16 for Nintendo Switch, and that it is also playable on Nintendo Switch 2, according to the company's launch announcement ([Nintendo](https://www.nintendo.com/us/whatsnew/tomodachi-life-living-the-dream-direct-spotlights-quirky-fun-with-player-made-mii-characters-game-launches-on-nintendo-switch-april-16/)). Nintendo also describes the game as the first new Tomodachi Life entry in more than 10 years and says players can build an island life around custom Mii characters ([Nintendo](https://www.nintendo.com/us/whatsnew/tomodachi-life-living-the-dream-direct-spotlights-quirky-fun-with-player-made-mii-characters-game-launches-on-nintendo-switch-april-16/)).

## What We Know

- In Nintendo's *Ask the Developer* interview, director Ryutaro Takahashi said development started around 2017, after Miitomo had settled down, and that he and producer Yoshio Sakamoto wanted to make a new Tomodachi Life after they had already "squeezed all we could" from the previous 3DS game ([Nintendo](https://www.nintendo.com/us/whatsnew/ask-the-developer-vol-21-tomodachi-life-living-the-dream-part-1/)).
- Nintendo says the team rebuilt the series from the ground up for this release and added a new text-to-speech engine for the Switch-era Mii characters ([Nintendo](https://www.nintendo.com/us/whatsnew/ask-the-developer-vol-21-tomodachi-life-living-the-dream-part-1/)).
- TechRadar reported that Takahashi framed the project around user-generated content and said the game took roughly nine years to develop ([TechRadar](https://www.techradar.com/gaming/nintendo/tomodachi-life-living-the-dream-took-roughly-nine-years-to-develop-and-focuses-on-user-generated-content-for-infinite-ways-to-enjoy-the-game-developer-reveals)).
